title: His Answer To A Friend.
content:
You ask me what I do, and how I live?
    And, noble friend, this answer I must give:
    Drooping, I draw on to the vaults of death,
    O'er which you'll walk, when I am laid beneath.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "His Answer To A Friend.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
